title: Chapter 7 Quiz: User Input and Forms
description: Test your knowledge of TextInput, form state, validation, keyboard handling, and advanced inputs.

questions.question: Which prop shows a numeric keypad on TextInput?
questions.explanation: `keyboardType="numeric"` enables the numeric keypad.

questions.question: How do you make a TextInput secure (for password)?
questions.explanation: `secureTextEntry` hides the entered text.

questions.question: What is a common pattern for managing many form fields?
options: A single state object with all values
questions.explanation: A single object groups related state and reduces repetition.

questions.question: When should validation typically occur?
options: On submit and optionally on blur/change
questions.explanation: Validate on submit for final check, and optionally on blur for immediate feedback.

questions.question: What does `KeyboardAvoidingView` do?
options: Adjusts layout to keep inputs visible when keyboard opens
questions.explanation: It shifts its children to avoid being covered by the keyboard.

questions.question: How do you programmatically dismiss the keyboard?
questions.explanation: `Keyboard.dismiss()` dismisses the active keyboard.

questions.question: Which component is used for a boolean toggle?
questions.explanation: `Switch` is the standard toggle component.

questions.question: What library provides the Picker component?
questions.explanation: `@react-native-picker/picker` is the community picker.

questions.question: Which prop limits the number of characters in TextInput?
questions.explanation: `maxLength` restricts input length.

questions.question: What does `onSubmitEditing` do?
options: Called when the input is blurred
Called when the return key is pressed
Called when the input is focused
questions.explanation: `onSubmitEditing` fires when the user presses the submit button on the keyboard.

questions.question: TextInput must always be controlled with `value` and `onChangeText`.
questions.explanation: You can use uncontrolled inputs with `defaultValue`, but controlled is recommended.

questions.question: Switch uses `onValueChange` to handle changes.
questions.explanation: Yes, `onValueChange` is called with the new boolean value.

questions.question: The Picker component looks the same on iOS and Android.
questions.explanation: It renders a modal on iOS and a dropdown on Android by default.

questions.question: Client‑side validation is sufficient for security.
questions.explanation: Always validate on the server; client validation is for UX only.

questions.question: The prop used to set the label on the return key is _____.
questions.explanation: `returnKeyType` controls the return key label (done, go, etc.).

questions.question: To make a TextInput multiline, set _____ to true.
questions.explanation: `multiline={true}` enables multiple lines.

questions.question: Which component would you use to select a date?
options: Both A and B are valid
questions.explanation: Both platform‑specific and community pickers exist; the community one works cross‑platform.

questions.question: What does `keyboardShouldPersistTaps` control?
options: Whether the keyboard stays open when tapping outside
Whether taps should dismiss the keyboard
questions.explanation: It determines if tapping outside an input dismisses the keyboard.

questions.question: How can you clear an error when the user starts typing?
options: Use `onChangeText` to set that field's error to undefined
questions.explanation: You can clear a specific error in the change handler when the value becomes valid.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `textAlignVertical` on TextInput?
options: Aligns text vertically (especially on Android multiline)
questions.explanation: On Android, multiline text aligns top by default; `textAlignVertical: 'top'` ensures it's at the top.
